❯ The Claude Code Toolbox Contents Cheatsheet

A BookBank Field Guide · Built-in Tools, Explained

The Claude Code Toolbox

Claude Code doesn't "have powers" — it has tools: a fixed set of named capabilities it calls, each one checked against your permission rules. Learn what each tool really does, the foot-guns hiding inside it, and when Claude reaches for it.

❯ read, search, edit, run, delegate, schedule, extend.

written in the voice of a patient senior engineer · example first, then the why, then the gotchas

▶ Start reading ⚡ Cheatsheet

Table of Contents

  1. The Toolbox & the Permission Model What a "tool" is, the Yes/No prompt, and the ToolName(specifier) rule format. foundation
  2. Reading & Searching the Codebase Read, Glob, Grep, LSP — find files, scan contents, navigate types. read
  3. Changing Code Safely Edit, Write, NotebookEdit — and the read-before-edit contract. edit
  4. Running Commands Bash & PowerShell — persistence, timeouts, output limits, background jobs. run
  5. Reacting in the Background Monitor — tail logs, poll CI, watch files, subscribe to a WebSocket. run
  6. Delegating to Subagents Agent — a fresh context window that returns one result; tool inheritance. orchestrate
  7. Orchestrating: Workflows & Skills Workflow scripts that fan out many agents; Skill for reusable playbooks. orchestrate
  8. Planning, Tasks & Worktrees Plan mode, the Task* tools, TodoWrite, and isolated git worktrees. orchestrate
  9. Reaching the Web WebSearch finds, WebFetch reads — and why WebFetch is lossy by design. reach
  10. Scheduling & Staying in Touch Cron tasks, self-paced loops, push notifications, files, and Routines. reach
  11. Extending with MCP & Talking to You ToolSearch, MCP resources, and the tools Claude uses to ask and report. orchestrate
❯
About this book

Every chapter groups a family of tools, leads with a real call, then covers the behavior that bites people. Each tool is tagged no prompt or asks first — its default permission. Self-contained and offline; grounded in the official Claude Code tools reference (current to v2.1.196). Press ⌘R or reopen to refresh after edits.

⌂ Library
